111786.explanation: Although we strive for equal distribution, it is more important to form a quality team that properly reflects the election outcome. The voice of our residents is decisive in this.

111787.explanation: A referendum does not provide a complete picture of the opinions of the municipality's residents but answers a specific proposition. Important issues should be discussed with all residents from all perspectives. This way, all interests are taken into account, leading to better decisions.

111790.explanation: The agricultural sector must be given opportunities to maintain their income levels. The word “always” is too strong, but where possible it should be allowed. Expanding these opportunities ensures a varied offer in terms of recreation and local products.

111792.explanation: From day one, Gemeentebelangen has not supported shelter facilities of this scale. We respect the agreements made but do not want to extend them.

111793.explanation: Housing for migrant workers is the responsibility of employers. However, we believe that external housing options must also be provided when on-site accommodation is not feasible. Frameworks should be established for this (maximum number, integration with the environment, no nuisance).

111797.explanation: If the national fireworks ban comes into effect, the need for a festive moment does not disappear. Gemeentebelangen wants to allocate a budget so that a show can be organized per village, for example by an association. The money may also be used for a New Year's Eve party.

111801.explanation: There is a high demand for housing. In our municipality, we must look everywhere possible to meet this need. However, it is important that there remains enough space for the agricultural sector. Housing in unsuitable locations hinders the future of the agricultural sector and must be prevented.

111802.explanation: Gemeentebelangen finds it important that everyone participates in our community and also believes sports are healthy. Making sports accessible may be supported through the child allowance scheme and within existing income support arrangements.

111805.explanation: Music is important in children's development. Gemeentebelangen supports introducing music to children in a positive way, without obligations. The child allowance scheme may possibly offer a solution in this regard.

111807.explanation: Monuments show the history of our municipality, so according to Gemeentebelangen, further budget cuts are definitely not appropriate.

111809.explanation: Our municipality is recognized as a safe municipality in various studies. If there are bottlenecks, we certainly want to invest in enforcement, but if it is not necessary, we prefer to keep municipal taxes as low as possible.

111810.explanation: The cameras at the station in Dalfsen may be used. Furthermore, we only support camera surveillance when there is structural nuisance.

111811.explanation: Gemeentebelangen strongly supports starter loans to help our youth obtain housing within our municipality. This helps preserve our culture and liveability.

111814.explanation: Gemeentebelangen advocates transforming recreational homes located outside of official recreation parks. Recreation should remain because it is a real added value for our municipality.

111815.explanation: There is a great need for housing, especially for starters and seniors. Where possible, we should focus on infill development, splitting, and other options to add housing, but this will not solve the large demand for homes among our residents. Expansion remains necessary!

111816.explanation: In our municipality, everyone participates and contributes to the community. This means that an appropriate reciprocal contribution (matching individual capabilities) may be required.

111818.explanation: Gemeentebelangen finds the current policy boundary of support up to 120% of the minimum income acceptable. However, there is still hidden poverty, and efforts should be made to identify and help this group towards independence.

111820.explanation: The current policy regarding charging stations in neighborhoods is sufficient. In case of shortages, rapid action must be taken to add more stations.

111821.explanation: Almost the entire municipality already has a 30 km/h speed limit except for some access roads. As long as it can be done safely and responsibly, these roads should remain at 50 km/h.

111822.explanation: Gemeentebelangen values appropriate care provision, but maximum reimbursements should be set. The municipality must pay costs within an acceptable distance. Extra costs related to religion, culture, or belief are at the individual's own expense.

111823.explanation: Gemeentebelangen expects healthcare to be provided with quality at a reasonable and acceptable price. We also want research conducted to prevent abuse and fraud. High-quality and affordable youth care remains important.

111824.explanation: If it is possible to lower taxes, that is a good thing. We do want to maintain public services. Gemeentebelangen looks at the long-term financial perspective.

111828.explanation: Gemeentebelangen places residents first. This means there is no room for additional priority for specific target groups. The pressure on the housing market also causes problems for our own residents.

111829.explanation: The polluter pays. Agreeing with this principle means collective costs will rise, including municipal levies. It must be made (financially) simple to dispose of waste streams close to home.

111832.explanation: School swimming is no longer offered by schools. Allocating money for this is therefore pointless. Swimming lessons should be and must remain accessible to everyone. We want to support this through the child allowance scheme or existing income support.

111833.explanation: Dialect is important as cultural heritage. Attention to this is important according to Gemeentebelangen. Encouraging and acknowledging it is fine, but making it mandatory goes too far for Gemeentebelangen.

111834.explanation: Gemeentebelangen does not see how a coffeeshop contributes to the liveability of our municipality.

111836.explanation: Gemeentebelangen finds it important that we remain a rural municipality in appearance, which does not fit with high-rise buildings. Building for our own residents in a way that suits our municipality means no tall residential towers.

111838.explanation: Gemeentebelangen wants to honor existing agreements, but if it is possible to do so by enlarging existing windmills or generating renewable energy otherwise, that is also acceptable. Gemeentebelangen wants not only the burdens but also the benefits locally.

111839.explanation: Paper collection is currently done by associations in the municipality, and they also generate income from this. An extra container requires space at homes and reduces income for the associations.

111786.explanation: It is especially important that the college forms a good team. A team composed based on skills, backgrounds, and personalities. We do prefer that the new college includes both men and women (but it is not a requirement).

111787.explanation: Based on good principles, a referendum can contribute to public debate. With the result, the college and municipal council could make better decisions. Question formulation, timing, and function (advisory/binding) are important conditions that require careful consideration.

111790.explanation: We support new initiatives from the agricultural sector for a broader or different revenue model, such as home sales, recreation, and other social functions, fitting the character of Dalfsen.

111792.explanation: We have both a legal obligation under the Distribution Act and a moral obligation to contribute to asylum shelter. With long-term asylum shelter, we provide peace and clarity for all involved.

111793.explanation: Migrant workers have become indispensable for some of our companies. We therefore think along with companies about creating sufficient, livable housing options for migrant workers. How and where this housing takes place is a subject of discussion.

111797.explanation: If initiatives emerge from the villages to organize a central fireworks show, the municipality can act as a facilitator/supporter. Organizing a fireworks show itself is not seen as a municipal task by us.

111801.explanation: During the past period, CDA successfully advocated for a second business home for agricultural companies on the plot. This also applies to extra options for temporary housing and pre-caregiver homes on private grounds.

111802.explanation: We find it important that residents receiving social assistance benefits can actively participate in Dalfsen. The municipality actively supports residents with social assistance benefits, and we want this to continue.

111805.explanation: Culture connects and brings residents together. We continue to invest in cultural education. Music lessons outside school hours are basically an individual responsibility.

111807.explanation: Our identity is rooted in the villages, the people, and the traditions that connect us. From the regional language to church heritage, from monumental farms to the charming streets in our villages: we cherish what makes us unique.

111809.explanation: Dalfsen is one of the safest municipalities in the country. We are proud of how the existing enforcement officers, together with neighborhood police officers and active neighborhood apps, ensure safety within the municipality of Dalfsen.

111810.explanation: Additional camera surveillance should not be an end in itself and also brings objections, such as extra costs and infringements on privacy.

111811.explanation: The CDA considers the starter loan a proven successful scheme. Therefore, we want to maintain this scheme to help starters get started in this difficult housing market.

111814.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreational (i.e., temporary) stays. We do respect existing rights that were obtained in the past. For problem situations, however, we actively seek a solution (tailored approach) to prevent residents from getting into trouble.

111815.explanation: Expansion (beyond existing village boundaries) remains necessary to build enough homes. However, we focus on more flexible policies for infill locations (building sites within the built-up area) with social added value as the main principle.

111816.explanation: Work provides satisfaction, purpose, and a social network. Therefore, we encourage active participation of all residents, taking into account everyone’s possibilities and capabilities. If employment is not feasible, we focus on participation through volunteer work.

111818.explanation: The municipality of Dalfsen already plays an active role in the subsistence security of vulnerable residents. We take responsibility for subsistence security by taking over debts at certain moments. Nationally, we advocate for simplifying the tax and benefits system.

111820.explanation: We follow (national) guidelines and take into account future mobility in both existing and new neighborhoods, where (more) charging spots are part of the public space of the future.

111821.explanation: A lower speed does not automatically lead to higher traffic safety. The maximum speed must fit the function and design of the road.

111822.explanation: Freedom of choice is important for everyone, but at the same time other aspects such as costs must also be considered to keep care affordable for everyone.

111823.explanation: Price is one of the aspects but should not be leading. Youth care must above all be of good quality and accessible to the residents who need it.

111824.explanation: With the proceeds from the property tax (ozb), the municipality of Dalfsen invests in the future, among other things through all public facilities and the public space.

111828.explanation: Residents who have been on the waiting list for a rental home for many years will have to wait even longer due to giving priority to status holders. On the other hand, we also believe that Dalfsen must comply with national agreements to provide a place for status holders.

111829.explanation: As CDA, we stand for 'the polluter pays.' Free disposal does not mean no costs are incurred. These costs are passed on to all residents. Sufficient collection points for waste are therefore a plus.

111832.explanation: Every resident of the municipality of Dalfsen must have the opportunity to learn to swim and become sufficiently skilled. We consider a sufficient supply of (affordable) swimming lessons more important than collective school swimming.

111833.explanation: Culture connects and brings residents together. We continue to invest in cultural education. Attention can also be given to our regional language.

111834.explanation: Coffeeshops do not fit the character of the municipality of Dalfsen. A coffeeshop tends to have a negative, attracting effect with possible associated nuisance.

111836.explanation: High-rise buildings require a tailored approach and can be up to 4-5 stories in certain village locations, provided they fit well into the surroundings.

111838.explanation: The province has taken control over the development of new wind turbines. Regarding any plans for wind turbines, we want the municipality to play an active role, with a focus on 100% local ownership and/or co-production with the sustainable villages. It could therefore only be considered under favorable conditions.

111839.explanation: Paper collection has traditionally taken place via community organizations, which derive some income from this to keep membership fees affordable.

111786.explanation: Although men and women are equal, a proportional distribution is not a goal in itself.

111787.explanation: Council members are elected representatives and have a mandate from that role to represent the residents. The ChristenUnie supports active participation and the use of a citizens’ assembly in important cases.

111790.explanation: It is good to offer the agricultural sector an alternative source of income as they downscale their businesses. This can sometimes be through a shop or campsite, but this is always tailored and must fit the environment.

111792.explanation: The ChristenUnie is proud and grateful that since October 1st, structural shelter has been realized within the municipality. It fits the vision that every person counts and that we must be generous towards displaced people.

111793.explanation: In practice, housing of labor migrants often goes wrong. Abuses concerning housing and exploitation of seasonal workers must be prevented. This requires coordination between employers and the municipality.

111797.explanation: In combination with permitting a few locations where carbide shooting is allowed. Suitable for the municipality’s budget. A national fireworks ban must also include enforcement.

111801.explanation: This is already possible within current rules. However, the municipality must retain control over the number and size.

111802.explanation: The ChristenUnie focuses on grassroots sport: sport for everyone, nearby and affordable. For those who cannot afford it, there is a sports fund. We want to maintain this.

111805.explanation: Music is important. Like sports, we want to promote accessibility, but not necessarily for people who can pay for it themselves.

111807.explanation: We cherish our rich local history. It helps us remember where we come from. This is embedded in culture, historic sites, and monuments.

111809.explanation: The ChristenUnie considers social safety important. BOAs (special enforcement officers) can contribute positively to that.

111810.explanation: Under strict conditions and in specific locations, camera surveillance is considered discussable by the CU.

111811.explanation: We commit ourselves to giving starters space in the housing market. Besides starter loans, we also seek other instruments such as duo-buy (buyer purchases only the house, not the land) and guaranteed purchase (buying at a discount).

111814.explanation: Simply converting a holiday home into a permanent residence is not our preference.

111815.explanation: The built-up area is the designated place for housing construction. Outside the built-up area, the land has another function.

111816.explanation: That is, at least, realistically possible. Not everyone is capable of this. So, there are exceptions.

111818.explanation: The ChristenUnie finds it important that people in our municipality do not fall below the poverty line. Besides families and singles, children are also a concern. Poverty often leads to social problems, poorer (school) performance, and stress. We want to combat this.

111820.explanation: In the coming years, the use of electric cars will continue to rise. Not everyone has a private charging spot.

111821.explanation: Pedestrians and cyclists must be given more space in traffic and be able to participate safely.

111822.explanation: Care should be close, people-oriented, and easily accessible. Churches and other institutions can strengthen the government, but their callings must remain separate.

111823.explanation: Price is important, but even more important is that youth care can be provided locally and that it is professional and reliable.

111824.explanation: If the municipality structurally has a budget surplus, the property tax (OZB) does not need to be increased.

111828.explanation: Certain groups have an urgency declaration in the allocation of social rental housing. This declaration is granted in special circumstances. People with an urgency declaration receive priority.

111829.explanation: By properly sorting waste, it becomes a raw material. We encourage more advanced waste separation, which also requires appropriate measures. Not all waste streams need to be free of charge, but for some streams, it can be practical. Consider chemical waste to ensure separate collection.

111832.explanation: Sports and physical activity are healthy. We want to promote this broadly. Swimming is an example. We find it important that children and adults can swim and want to encourage this. After all, Dalfsen has open water in many places.

111833.explanation: History and cultural heritage are important in defining the identity of our region.

111836.explanation: Where it can be integrated into the environment, we consider 5 residential floors with underground parking. This creates more green space and room in the neighborhood. Of course, not an entire neighborhood full of this, but in some places where it fits well.

111838.explanation: in combination with a municipal energy company.

111839.explanation: Certainly if the proceeds go to good causes. This is already the case in many places.

111786.explanation: The municipal government should represent the people living in our municipality well. When feasible, this means an equal number of men and women. Such a government includes all perspectives to make fair decisions.

111787.explanation: Good participation requires dialogue and prior input, not simplification of complex issues through referenda afterwards. For very clear choices (think of the tree-cutting at Rechterensedijk in the past), a referendum can be a last resort for the council to gather advice.

111790.explanation: Side activities can contribute to farmers' revenue models but must fit with the environment, nature, and livability.

111792.explanation: Dalfsen is a welcoming municipality and takes responsibility for humane and well-organized shelter.

111793.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing for seasonal workers to prevent abuses and nuisance.

111797.explanation: A central fireworks show increases safety, reduces nuisance, and brings people together.

111801.explanation: Unrestrained housing construction in rural areas harms the landscape and nature and leads to fragmentation.

111802.explanation: Sports promote health and participation; money should never be a barrier to participation.

111805.explanation: Music education contributes to development and wellbeing and should be accessible to every child.

111807.explanation: Monuments are cultural heritage and contribute to our identity and tourism.

111809.explanation: Safety requires a balance between enforcement, prevention, and social approaches. Cameras are inappropriate in most places in our municipality. We prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

111810.explanation: Safety requires a balance between enforcement, prevention, and social approaches. Cameras are inappropriate in most places in our municipality. We prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

111811.explanation: Helping starters is important, but structural affordable housing is more effective than financial band-aids. We want to reduce the debt burden for our residents by building cheaper homes with municipal funds so less borrowing is needed.

111814.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

111815.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the greenery around our living areas as best as possible. But to tackle the housing shortage, we must also use agricultural land for housing. This is also an opportunity to create pleasant, green neighborhoods for our residents.

111816.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

111818.explanation: More and more people with low incomes fall outside schemes; the threshold should be raised so more people can count on support. Especially when you can't make ends meet financially despite working.

111820.explanation: Charging stations are important but should not come at the expense of scarce parking space without clear consideration.

111821.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

111822.explanation: Appropriate care means, in principle, freedom of choice and respect for background and beliefs where feasible. However, this is not always possible.

111823.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

111824.explanation: Municipal revenues are needed for services; costs must be fairly distributed. We oppose annual increases in the property tax without visible investments benefiting our residents.

111828.explanation: Quick housing helps status holders integrate and prevents long-term shelter. Many more target groups get priority. We simply need to build more to provide space for all groups.

111829.explanation: Free access to the recycling center encourages proper waste separation and prevents dumping.

111832.explanation: School swimming increases safety and ensures equal opportunities for all children.

111833.explanation: Attention to the Sallands dialect strengthens culture and identity.

111834.explanation: This is not a goal in itself. Establishing a coffeeshop requires local support and must demonstrably contribute to safety and livability. Without concrete necessity, this is not a priority.

111836.explanation: High-rise buildings can be allowed sometimes but must fit the village scale and environment. We think of a maximum of 5 floors in new building locations (not infill) allowing for cheaper housing, more green space, and underground parking.

111838.explanation: Wind energy is necessary for the energy transition but only when realized by our own residents and the income remains in our municipality.

111839.explanation: Good waste separation is important, but the best solution varies per area. We see paper collection as a good source of income for our associations, which manage these containers themselves. New containers must meet a need and not reduce associations' income.

111786.explanation: The executive board is not an elected body, so initially gender is not the focus but competencies. When equally qualified, this is a good starting point.

111787.explanation: D66 Dalfsen supports a citizen's assembly; a group of randomly selected citizens who discuss an important topic and advise the council.

111790.explanation: Farmers should have space for side activities such as a shop or campsite. This provides extra income, reduces dependence, and helps transition to a sustainable and future-proof agriculture.

111792.explanation: D66 Dalfsen is neither for closing nor opening asylum centers. As long as asylum shelter is needed and people require protection, and within the law, we take responsibility with attention to support and good shelter.

111793.explanation: Good housing for seasonal workers is essential for dignified work and livability. D66 does not choose a fixed model but supports clear quality requirements, supervision, and cooperation between employers, municipality, and surroundings. Custom solutions per situation are paramount.

111797.explanation: A municipal fireworks show sounds appealing but in a municipality with many villages leads to high costs or unequal choices. Community funds are not meant for this; they are better spent on facilities that have value throughout the year.

111801.explanation: Farmers should be given the space to build small-scale homes on their land as part of a future-proof business. Not on every farmyard, but with direction and customization: only in places that truly add value to the village, landscape, and livability.

111805.explanation: Music lessons for every primary school child are valuable for development and equal opportunities. At the same time, this requires significant investments. The municipality can explore this step by step, focusing on reach and cooperation with schools and associations.

111807.explanation: Monument preservation protects our heritage and the identity of villages and communities. Budget cuts cause irreversible damage and are often more expensive in the long term. Proper maintenance prevents decay and keeps history alive.

111809.explanation: Dalfsen is a safe municipality, but the visibility of enforcement remains important. Now that there is no fixed police station, additional community officers can contribute to supervision, approachability, and a feeling of safety in neighborhoods and villages.

111810.explanation: Additional camera surveillance is not currently necessary in Dalfsen. The municipality is relatively safe, and camera surveillance interferes with privacy. D66 prefers visible people in the neighborhood and prevention over permanent control.

111811.explanation: Dalfsen already offers a starter loan for homebuyers purchasing a house for the first time. Continuing and sustaining this support helps starters in the housing market, especially now that barriers are high and recent extra funds have been made available.

111814.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes is a liberal and practical choice. It helps alleviate the housing shortage and aligns with changing tourist use, provided homes meet clear requirements for safety and quality of living.

111815.explanation: Building only within village boundaries is too limited. The housing shortage requires more space, also beyond those boundaries. However, priority is given to infill development where possible, careful expansion where necessary, with protection of the landscape and a green buffer towards Zwolle.

111816.explanation: Not everyone on social assistance can simply do social work due to health, care, or personal circumstances. D66 chooses customization and guidance towards participation instead of an obligation that puts more pressure on people.

111818.explanation: Dalfsen is a relatively affluent municipality and concerns a small group of residents. Precisely for that reason, a slight increase in the income limit can be appropriate: targeted, affordable, and effective for those just falling outside the support net.

111820.explanation: Already, more than 40% of new cars are electric. To enable this transition, the municipality must provide sufficient charging points. This prevents charging stress, supports sustainable transport, and makes electric driving feasible for everyone.

111821.explanation: 30 km/h is appropriate and desirable in residential streets and living areas for safety and livability. On through roads, customization is needed. D66 opts for targeted implementation, not a generic speed reduction.

111822.explanation: Choice in healthcare is important, but in a rural area with limited providers, this is not always feasible. The municipality can encourage care to align as much as possible with a person's background, within what is practical and available.

111823.explanation: Quality must be paramount in youth care. Price is important but should never be decisive. Good care requires craftsmanship, continuity, and trust – which weigh more than the lowest cost.

111824.explanation: The current property tax (OZB) is balanced and contributes to strong facilities. Reduction is not necessary: those with more property contribute more. This keeps important public services affordable and fairly distributed.

111828.explanation: The housing of status holders is a statutory task. The municipality carries this out carefully within national rules, with attention to support, local ties, and fair distribution of scarce homes. It is not about giving priority, but about taking responsibility.

111829.explanation: Free disposal of waste at the recycling center lowers the threshold to properly separate waste and prevents littering. Waste is increasingly becoming a resource; this fits a circular approach and aligns with broader waste policy.

111832.explanation: Learning to swim is important, but school swimming puts additional pressure on schools that already have many tasks. D66 prefers other, more effective ways to promote swimming skills without making it a standard part of education.

111833.explanation: The Sallands dialect is valuable cultural heritage, but basic proficiency in Dutch is under pressure and deserves priority. Attention to dialect is possible, but not as a compulsory part and not at the expense of language development.

111834.explanation: Allowing a coffeeshop fits a realistic and liberal policy. It provides regulation, reduces nuisance, and removes soft drugs from illegality. With clear rules, this can be integrated safely and responsibly.

111836.explanation: Dalfsen is not a place for high apartment buildings, but somewhat taller construction than now is possible. With 4 to 6 residential floors, we can build more efficiently, enable parking under buildings, and use less space, while preserving the village scale.

111838.explanation: More wind turbines are needed to meet legal climate targets and agreements on sustainable energy. Through careful location choices and local ownership, Dalfsen can take responsibility and maintain support.

111839.explanation: Additional containers for old paper are not needed. These facilities already exist in Dalfsen. The focus should be on proper use and maintenance of the existing collection system.

111786.explanation: For the VVD, quality comes first; we choose the most suitable candidates based on talent and experience, not on quotas.

111787.explanation: We choose good explanation and targeted participation where meaningful, but not referenda. Council members are elected with a four-year mandate. Accountability takes place during elections.

111790.explanation: We support farmers who want to diversify with side activities such as farm shops or recreation.

111792.explanation: We choose distribution across communities and small-scale to prevent overload and promote integration. Dalfsen has a reception center, and Ukrainians should be spread over the other communities, as agreed.

111793.explanation: This fits within the entrepreneurs' own responsibility and relieves the regular housing market.

111797.explanation: We prefer to support local association initiatives rather than the government executing these themselves.

111801.explanation: We support initiatives that contribute to the housing task, provided they fit within the frameworks.

111802.explanation: The heaviest shoulders bear the heaviest burdens; we support those who really cannot manage alone.

111805.explanation: We want to strengthen cooperation between schools and culture for broad development.

111807.explanation: Preserving character is important, but we must always critically assess the efficiency of expenditures.

111809.explanation: More police presence on the street, not more community officers.

111810.explanation: Safety is a prerequisite for freedom; cameras can help in risk areas.

111811.explanation: We want young people and starters to be able to live in their own community.

111814.explanation: We are open to flexible living arrangements, provided they do not harm livability or recreational offerings.

111815.explanation: Infill development is preferred, but expansion is necessary to meet housing demand.

111816.explanation: Those who can participate, do participate; we expect a contribution according to ability.

111818.explanation: Work must pay off. No shifting of the income threshold.

111820.explanation: Sustainability measures must be practical and feasible for residents.

111821.explanation: We choose 30 km/h within built-up areas for safety, 50 km/h for ring roads, and 60 km/h outside built-up areas. On through roads and provincial roads 80 km/h. Four-lane roads 100 km/h.

111822.explanation: The freedom to make your own choices and customized care are essential to us.

111823.explanation: Help must be humane, effective, and local; quality and results take precedence over the lowest price.

111824.explanation: As long as there are multi-million surpluses, money should be returned to residents rather than being saved up.

111828.explanation: Of course, we want to give our own youth a good chance at housing, but we also have a duty to our new residents.

111829.explanation: We apply the polluter/user pays principle to keep general taxes low.

111832.explanation: This contributes to the safety and broad development of children. The Netherlands is a water country. Being able to swim well is essential.

111833.explanation: We cherish our local culture and the identity of our village communities.

111834.explanation: We focus on prevention and education against drug use, especially among young people.

111836.explanation: Residential flats are allowed in Dalfsen. As tall as necessary to keep housing affordable, but considering the appearance. We consider affordable housing very important. In some situations, building higher than 4 residential floors is the way to achieve this.

111838.explanation: We have an obligation from the national government to meet regional energy targets. We prefer to do this on industrial sites to protect the open landscape.

111839.explanation: We support practical solutions that improve services to residents. Even better would be one recycling container per household to limit the proliferation of containers.

111786.explanation: The quality, experience, and suitability of candidate aldermen should be leading in forming the college, not gender.

111787.explanation: By actively involving residents in major decisions, the municipality increases support and trust in the administration.

111790.explanation: Flexibility remains important: side activities can be possible, but only when carefully assessed for impact and support.

111792.explanation: By not establishing shelter locations, the municipality can focus on core tasks like livability, housing construction, and support for existing residents.

111793.explanation: Housing for seasonal workers is important, but mandatory housing on the company's own premises is not always feasible or desirable.

111797.explanation: Dalfsen is geographically widely spread, so a central fireworks show would only be visible to a small portion of residents.

111801.explanation: A generic permit or a generic ban is too black-and-white; customization offers the best balance between agricultural interests and spatial planning.

111802.explanation: There are already schemes such as the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture and other income-supporting provisions that can help residents without the municipality having to cover all costs fully.

111805.explanation: Schools and cultural institutions can often already offer low-threshold music activities through cooperation without requiring full municipal subsidies.

111807.explanation: By critically reviewing current expenditures, the municipality can create room for other important tasks such as livability, housing, or social support.

111809.explanation: Dalfsen is a relatively safe municipality; extra enforcement officers are not always the most effective or necessary solution.

111810.explanation: Camera surveillance involves costs and requires careful consideration regarding privacy and proportionality.

111811.explanation: The municipality should focus better on sufficient affordable housing and good flow-through, rather than on financial instruments that distort the market.

111814.explanation: Allowing permanent residence can help relieve the housing shortage, especially in a municipality with a high demand for affordable housing.

111815.explanation: By opting for in-filling and densification, the municipality can strengthen existing centers and better utilize amenities.

111816.explanation: Socially meaningful work can contribute to participation, structure, and increasing chances of returning to paid employment.

111818.explanation: The need for support varies greatly per household; a generic increase does not always do justice to individual situations.

111820.explanation: Designating more parking spaces as charging points limits available parking for residents without electric cars.

111821.explanation: In some streets, 30 km/h can contribute to safety and livability, while 50 km/h is perfectly responsible on other roads.

111822.explanation: Organizing a broad offering based on religion, culture, or belief can lead to higher costs and extra pressure on the care budget.

111823.explanation: Looking only at price is too limited: quality, expertise, and continuity are at least as important in youth care.

111824.explanation: The municipality must handle tax pressure carefully and critically review its own expenses before increasing charges.

111828.explanation: Automatically giving priority to permit holders is unfair to residents who have been waiting for social housing for years.

111829.explanation: A small contribution helps cover processing and personnel costs and prevents the burden from falling entirely on general funds.

111832.explanation: School swimming contributes to children's swimming skills and safety, especially in a water-rich environment like Dalfsen.

111833.explanation: By giving schools the freedom to implement this in their own way, flexibility remains without the municipality imposing a heavy program.

111834.explanation: There are sufficient alternatives in the region, making a local coffeeshop not a necessary facility.

111836.explanation: In some areas, densification with taller buildings can contribute to housing challenges, while in other places it does not fit the village character of Dalfsen.

111838.explanation: The nuisance to nearby residents, such as noise, shadow flicker, and obstruction of views, is significant and often underestimated.

111839.explanation: A container can offer convenience, but also involves costs, management, and risk of pollution.

statements.title: There should be a container for old paper.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Dalfsen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Dalfsen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Dalfsen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
There should be more camera surveillance in Dalfsen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should enable all primary schools to give attention to the Sallands dialect.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: High-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Dalfsen.
More wind turbines should be installed in the municipality of Dalfsen during the next council term.

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.
These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
